task_completion.description: Write logic to read the HEC-RAS HDF5 geometry and result data.

task_completion.description: Implement the logic to calculate bankfull discharge or set it to 0 if it never floods.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Python script contains conditional logic comparing water surface elevation (WSE) to minimum bank elevation, and extracting flow (Q) on the rising limb, including a fallback to set Bankfull Q to 0 if Max WSE < Min Bank Elev.

task_completion.description: Implement logic to export the summary CSV with the required columns.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Python script contains code (e.g., using pandas or csv module) to write to '/home/ga/Documents/hec_ras_results/bankfull_capacity.csv' with the exact columns: 'RiverStation', 'Min_Bank_Elev_ft', 'Bankfull_Q_cfs', and 'Max_WSE_ft'.

task_completion.description: Execute the script to generate the final CSV file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The terminal shows the Python script being executed without errors, and/or a subsequent command (like 'cat' or opening in an editor) shows the generated 'bankfull_capacity.csv' file populated with the correct headers and data rows.
